turn-3-reveal.text: Good. 'I know' is an exception: sé.

turn-5-prompt.text: How would you say 'I don't know'?

turn-7-prompt.text: How would you say 'I don't know what he wants'? Remember, 'what' is 'qué'.

turn-9-reveal.text: Good. If you want to emphasize 'he', you can say '

turn-9-prompt.text: how would you say 'she sees'? Remember 'to see' is 'ver'.

turn-13-reveal.text: No quiero invitarlo, pero ella sí. Now, 'to leave' or 'to go out' is 'salir'.

turn-15-prompt.text: How do you say 'I'm going out to eat'?

turn-17-prompt.text: How would you say 'I have to do it'? Remember 'to do' is 'hacer'.

turn-19-reveal.text: Now, let's look at verbs with the '-go' ending like 'tengo', 'vengo', 'salgo'.

turn-21-prompt.text: How would you say 'I must see her'?

turn-23-prompt.text: how would you say 'I'm going to try to eat later'?

turn-25-full.text: Voy a intentar comer más tarde.
Excellent. Let's recap these useful verbs: 'quiero', 'voy a', 'tengo que', 'puedo', 'debo'. They allow you to easily add another verb after them.

outro-1-full.text: You've learned about irregular verbs like 'saber' and 'salir', and how to use them in sentences.

outro-2-full.text: Try creating your own sentences using these verbs. For example, 'I want to go out' or 'I must know'.

outro-3-full.text: These verbs are essential for everyday conversation. Practice using them to build your confidence.
